newsSince: No material company-specific news since June 9; the Q1 2026 beat (revenue $1,078M, non-GAAP EPS $2.30) and Q2 guide are prior. The Malaysia capacity ramp and ~$165-180B 2027 WFE framing remain intact but are not new. The +21% move since June 9 is price/sentiment driven (semicap re-rate), not fresh fundamentals.

verdicts.rankRationale: Latent supply-chain lens: foundational semicap/packaging supplier; thesis intact. No new evidence since June 9 (Q1 beat already known). The +21% move is sentiment, not fundamentals (and the pipeline applies the price re-rate separately), so hold the alpha score.
verdicts.bull: Vacuum/photonics/process-control and advanced-packaging tools lever AI fab and HBM capacity; Atotech deleveraging optionality.
verdicts.neutral: Cycle/leverage concerns persist; sharp run-up already discounts recovery.
verdicts.bear: Leverage and order-timing risk; +131% YTD compresses residual upside.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Semicap/HBM capex upcycle and Atotech carve-out
verdicts.primaryRisk: Leverage and lumpy WFE order timing

verdicts.rankRationale: AI-node lens: WFE-exposure thesis intact and marginally well-supported by the $165-180B 2027 WFE framing and Malaysia ramp, but none of that is new since June 9. Small positive drift, no re-rank; export-control risk is a standing caveat.
verdicts.bull: Direct leverage to AI-driven fab/HBM capacity additions via core WFE subsystems; capacity expansion supports share gains.
verdicts.neutral: Thesis intact but order timing and export-control overhang temper conviction.
verdicts.bear: Export-control exposure and WFE cyclicality could clip the AI-capex tailwind.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: AI fab / HBM capacity additions lifting WFE demand
